businessSummary.oneLiner: Government and enterprise AI data analytics platform leader, powered by Gotham (defense), Foundry (enterprise), and AIP (AI platform)
businessSummary.description: Palantir builds AI-powered data analysis platforms for government and enterprise clients. FY2025 revenue $4.48B (+60%). US commercial revenue exceeded $650M (Q4 +137% YoY). Pentagon adopted Maven AI as an official program of record for military-wide deployment. Selected as software developer for the $185B Golden Dome missile defense project. Palantir tools are in active use during the Iran conflict, with CNBC calling it 'defense tech's opportunity'.
howTheyMakeMoney: Enterprise AI platform (Foundry) - large-scale data integration
AIP (AI Platform) - generative AI integration platform
AIP Bootcamps - rapid deployment programs for customer conversion

keyMetrics.name: Trailing P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: 6.3x the S&P 500 IT sector avg of 34x. 4.8x the software sector avg of 45x. Extreme premium persists despite 3-day 12% drop

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E (FY2026E)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on FY2026 EPS estimate of $1.22. Stocktwits notes this is a 5-month low. Still 3x growth stock avg of 30-40x

keyMetrics.name: Revenue Growth (FY2026E)
keyMetrics.interpretation: FY2025 $4.48B to FY2026E $7.40B. Iran war escalation could further accelerate AI defense demand

keyMetrics.name: P/S (Price-to-Sales)
keyMetrics.interpretation: 5-7x premium vs software sector avg 10-15x. Slightly compressed by recent decline

keyMetrics.name: YTD Return
keyMetrics.interpretation: Down 22% year-to-date. 33.7% below 52-week high of $207.52. Lost 12% in just three days

keyMetrics.name: RSI
keyMetrics.interpretation: Approaching oversold territory (30). Three-day 12% plunge driving RSI lower. Technical bounce possible

growthDrivers.title: Stellantis 5-Year Partnership Renewal and Expansion (3/30)
growthDrivers.description: Renewed partnership with Stellantis, world's 4th largest automaker. Showcase for Foundry platform's enterprise manufacturing AI capabilities

growthDrivers.title: CNBC: 'Iran War Is Defense Tech's Opportunity, Palantir Tools in Active Use' (3/28)
growthDrivers.description: CNBC identifies Iran conflict as defense tech catalyst. Palantir holds billions in Pentagon contracts and CEO Karp confirms active military use

growthDrivers.title: Motley Fool: 'Palantir's Blockbuster Week' Three Key Developments (3/29)
growthDrivers.description: Golden Dome, Maven formal adoption, and Polymarket partnership named as the week's three blockbuster developments

growthDrivers.title: Golden Dome $185B Missile Defense Software (3/25)
growthDrivers.description: Palantir and Anduril co-developing Trump administration's $185B Golden Dome missile defense software. Houthi attacks heighten urgency

growthDrivers.title: Polymarket AI Fraud Prevention Partnership (3/26)
growthDrivers.description: Vergence AI powers Polymarket's trade surveillance and user behavior monitoring in real-time. Entry point into financial services

growthDrivers.title: Maven AI Full Military Adoption (Program of Record, 3/23)
growthDrivers.description: Pentagon designated Maven Smart System as official program of record. Long-term military-wide deployment confirmed. Battle-tested in Iran conflict

competitiveAdvantage.summary: 20+ year government defense AI track record, highest security clearances, Maven military-wide adoption, Iran war battle-tested. Moats money can't buy

moats.description: Ontology platform deeply embedded in government and military systems. Replacement cost is prohibitively high
Highest security clearances and 20+ year government trust. Virtually impossible for new entrants to match
Active use in Iran conflict for real-time targeting. War escalation via Houthi attacks increases demand
AIP bootcamps drive rapid adoption. Ontology data model creates expanding ecosystem

competitors.name: Anduril Industries
competitors.detail: Defense AI/drone startup. Co-develops Golden Dome with Palantir. $60B valuation

competitors.name: Snowflake / Databricks
competitors.detail: Enterprise data platform competitors. Far behind Palantir in government/defense access

competitors.name: Microsoft / AWS
competitors.detail: Cloud giants entering government AI. Cannot match Ontology's depth of integration

competitors.name: C3.ai
competitors.detail: Enterprise AI platform. Trails Palantir significantly in revenue scale and growth rate

recentNews.headline: Stocktwits: 'PLTR Loses Over 12% in Three Days: Is the Defense Boost Over?'
recentNews.significance: Three-day 12% decline gives back much of the war-driven rally. Forward P/E at 5-month low. Stocktwits sentiment turns bearish since mid-March

recentNews.headline: 247WallSt: 'Palantir Drops 4%. Can AI Partnerships Justify Top Valuation?'
recentNews.significance: 'Today's drop isn't about the business, it's about the price investors are willing to pay in a fear-driven environment.' $140 and $145 recovery levels are key

recentNews.headline: Parameter.io: 'Stellantis 5-Year Deal Expansion, Yet PLTR Falls 3.85% to $137.55'
recentNews.significance: Positive catalyst day saw -3.85% decline. After-hours another -0.81%. Macro selling pressure overwhelms bullish news

recentNews.headline: Barchart: 'Palantir Developing Golden Dome Core', Mizuho $195 Reaffirmed
recentNews.significance: Mizuho Securities reaffirms Outperform with $195 target. Revenue growth acceleration and margin expansion ahead. Golden Dome could reshape long-term defense architecture

recentNews.headline: TradingKey: 'PLTR -3.81%, Peter Thiel and Executives Continue March Selling'
recentNews.significance: Thiel and executives sold throughout March. 10b5-1 pre-planned, but negative psychological impact on sentiment

analystOpinions.notableComment: Mizuho $195 Outperform reaffirmed. MarketBeat consensus $198.59 (+44.4%). Stocktwits: 3-day 12% drop, defense rally fading

risks.title: 12% Drop in 3 Days: Defense Boost Fading
risks.description: Stocktwits reports 12% decline in three days, giving back much of the war-driven rally. Forward P/E at 5-month low. Sentiment turned bearish. Defense premium evaporating rapidly

risks.title: Extreme Valuation at P/E 215x
risks.description: P/E 215x is 6.3x IT sector average. Motley Fool calculates earnings need 330%+ growth to bring P/E down to 50x. War benefits already priced in

risks.title: Positive Catalysts Failing to Support Price
risks.description: Stellantis 5-year deal day saw -3.85%. After-hours another -0.81%. 247WallSt: 'It's about the price, not the business'

risks.title: Thiel and Executives Selling Throughout March
risks.description: TradingKey reports continuous March insider selling. Seeking Alpha argues 10b5-1 pre-planned, but market psychology suffers

risks.title: $100 Oil, Macro Tech Selloff
risks.description: Houthi attacks, oil $100+, inflation resurgence fears. Defense tailwind offset by broader high-multiple tech selling pressure

risks.title: Analyst Opinion Split
risks.description: 17 Buy, 14 Hold, 5 Sell. Market skepticism on valuation persists

buyReasons.title: 247WallSt: 'Macro Fear, Not Business Weakness' Driving Decline
buyReasons.rationale: Decline is risk-off driven, not fundamental. Macro stabilization could trigger rebound. Golden Dome, Maven, Stellantis catalysts still unreflected in price

buyReasons.title: Stellantis 5-Year Renewal + Iran War: Dual Defense/Enterprise Tailwind
buyReasons.rationale: Enterprise (Stellantis) and defense (Iran conflict) demand both accelerating. Golden Dome missile defense urgency rising with Houthi attacks

buyReasons.title: 34% Below Peak, RSI 38 Approaching Oversold
buyReasons.rationale: 52-week high $207 to current $137, 34% decline. RSI 38 nearing oversold. Three-day 12% may be overreaction

buyReasons.title: Consensus $199 (+44%), Mizuho $195 Outperform Reaffirmed
buyReasons.rationale: Decline widened upside to 44.4%. Barchart highlights Golden Dome Core role. Mizuho maintains conviction

overallOpinion: Down 12% in three days, giving back much of the war-driven defense rally. Stocktwits sentiment flipped bearish. Even a Stellantis 5-year deal announcement couldn't prevent a 3.85% slide on the same day

P/E 215x (4.8x software sector average). Thiel and executives selling all month, Barchart highlights Golden Dome Core role with Mizuho's $195 reaffirmed. 247WallSt nailed it: 'Not about the business, it's about what investors will pay in a fear-driven market'

Below 50-day ($149) and 200-day ($169). YTD -22%, 34% off peak. RSI 38 approaching oversold. Fundamentals are the strongest ever, but defense premium is fading fast. Hold, small DCA after macro stabilizes

investmentVerdict.summary: Three-day 12% drop signals the defense boost is fading. Golden Dome Core, Maven, and Stellantis make fundamentals the strongest ever, but P/E 215x and $100 oil block near-term upside. 34% off peak, RSI 38 nearing oversold. Defense premium evaporating, but macro calm could reignite. Small DCA after confirmation

witty.hook: Golden Dome, Maven, Stellantis. Down 12% in 3 days. The market reads the headlines and sells anyway
witty.text:
$PLTR $137.55(-3.85%)

Bullish news checklist:
Golden Dome Core. Check.
Maven full adoption. Check.
Stelantis 5-year renewal. Check.

Stock: -12% in 3 days.

1/4
thread:
247WallSt: 'Business is fine.'
'Price is the problem.'

P/E 215x. That's the whole explanation.

2/4

Defense boost is fading.
War continues but the premium evaporates.
Market wants the next catalyst.

3/4

Target $199 (+44%). RSI 38.
Buying at the bottom makes money.
Problem: is this the bottom? Hold.

4/4
